summary: OpenNori now records architecture apply records separately from Product AC evidence. In the settings-page smoke, architecture apply created .opennori/architecture/evidence/*.json with schema_valid=true and appeared in status/report, while AC-1 remained unknown until real Product AC evidence is recorded.

sources.label: npx vitest run test/cli-commands.test.js -t 'architecture apply command module|architecture baseline command module previews'
sources.command: npx vitest run test/cli-commands.test.js -t 'architecture apply command module|architecture baseline command module previews'

sources.label: npx vitest run test/core.test.js -t 'architecture apply records do not count|public JSON Schemas validate'
sources.command: npx vitest run test/core.test.js -t 'architecture apply records do not count|public JSON Schemas validate'

sources.label: architecture apply smoke: apply record visible, AC remains unknown
sources.command: architecture apply smoke: apply record visible, AC remains unknown

reviewability: Run the listed tests and smoke path; inspect status/report for architecture apply records and unchanged Product AC status.
limitations: Architecture apply records document baseline alignment; they intentionally do not prove user-facing acceptance by themselves.
